rationale_for_baseline_tier: A symmetric, afebrile, non-erythematous overuse flare of known mild seronegative RA can be managed with anti-inflammatories, warmth and rest, with prompt GP follow-up — a T2 baseline. The only escalation is for a single hot red joint/fever (septic arthritis).

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: A single joint becomes intensely red, hot, and agony to move, or a high fever with chills develops
escalation_edges.rationale: Suggests septic arthritis needing aspiration and IV antibiotics.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If any single joint becomes bright red, hot, and agony to move, or you get a high fever with chills, go to the emergency department.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Lives alone with acute hand disability tonight
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Ensure she can manage basic self-care; arrange help if needed.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is a flare of your mild arthritis from overusing your hands, not permanent damage. Use an anti-inflammatory with food, warm-water soaks, and rest your hands tomorrow. See your GP within a week for a long-term plan. Go to ED if one joint becomes bright red and hot and agony to move, or you get a high fever with chills.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: GP within 1 week; ED for a single hot joint/fever
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: No after-hours care needed unless a single hot joint or fever develops.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Telehealth review of a symmetric overuse flare of mild seronegative RA; septic arthritis screened negative (no single hot joint, afebrile); NSAIDs/warmth/rest advised; septic-arthritis safety-net given; rheumatology/DMARD discussion recommended.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Reassuring without a septic-arthritis screen or safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A superimposed septic joint is missed and destroys the joint / causes sepsis.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: ED/ambulance for a symmetric afebrile flare is over-triage.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot palpate joints or aspirate remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Use the symmetric/afebrile pattern and a single-hot-joint/fever screen to reassure, with a septic-arthritis safety-net.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Either ignore septic arthritis or over-triage a symmetric afebrile flare.
